meaning: adj.不适当的,不合适的,不正确的,不合礼仪的,不适宜
examples: It would be improper to comment at this stage.
Its image has been sullied by $9 billion in improper accounting.
The improper use of medicine could lead to severe adverse reactions.
Robert Torricelli, who was facing a federal investigation into improper gifts from a contributor.
Meanwhile, Wanderers striker Kevin Davies has been charged with improper conduct by the Football Association.
